signInInformation.title: Changes to Login Process

description.text:
We have merged the login process for EuroLeagueTV and EuroLeague ID – now a single account lets you access both, plus our Fantasy Challenge game.

**If you previously just had a EuroLeagueTV account, please use the forgot password function to activate your new Euroleague ID account.**

If you previously just had a Euroleague ID account, congratulations! You now also have access to a free tier of videos on EuroLeagueTV.\*

**If you are accessing our Fantasy Challenge via our partner's website, please be informed that our partners do not process, collect nor store any user data of participating users as stated in our Privacy Policy**

signInInformation.asteriskText: *EuroLeagueTV is available worldwide excluding Spain, France, Italy and Monaco.

accountDeletionInfo.text:
Before continuing to delete your EuroLeague ID account, please bear in mind the following:

If you play the Euroleague Fantasy Challenge, you may want to delete your team there first, as it will be more difficult to do that if your account no longer exists. You can do that at [euroleaguefantasy.euroleague.net/en/account](http://euroleaguefantasy.euroleague.net/en/account).

The legal basis for the processing is your consent. Only those third parties to whom Euroleague Properties SA is legally obliged and the companies entrusted with internal management services will have access to the data. You can exercise, at any time, under the terms established by law, your rights of access, rectification or deletion of data, as well as request restrictions on the processing of your personal data, oppose its processing or request the portability of your data, by sending a postal communication to the address indicated above or by writing an e-mail to data.protection.officer@euroleague.net. club.info: A signature team in European basketball, six-time champ Maccabi enters every season aiming for greatness. Over the years, Maccabi has relied on the tried-and-true formula of complementing the best Israeli players with talented, committed stars from abroad. Tal Brody, Mickey Berkowitz, Motti Aroesti, Kevin Magee, Doron Jamchy, Earl Williams, Aulcie Perry, Sarunas Jasikevicius, Anthony Parker and Nikola Vujcic belong to the elite of all-time European basketball greats. Maccabi won its first EuroLeague title in 1977 and did so again in 1981 with Berkowitz excelling. There was a wait until 2001 to lift the SuproLeague trophy and then the next title came soon after, in 2004. Maccabi became the first team to repeat as EuroLeague champion since Split in 1991 as Jasikevicius, Parker, Tal Burstein, Maceo Baston and Vujcic became a classic lineup in European basketball history. Maccabi got back to the championship game for the third year in a row in 2006, but CSKA stood in the way of a three-peat, and the Muscovites again triumphed in the 2008 title decider. Maccabi lost to Panathinaikos in the 2011 championship game before David Blatt led the club to glory in 2014. Since then Maccabi has not been back to the Final Four, but after making the playoffs three times in the last four years - and getting to a fifth game twice - Maccabi is again knocking on the door.

heroMedia.title: Josh Nebo slays former club as Milan beats battling Maccabi
heroMedia.summary: The big man posted a double-double to boost the Italian side’s postseason hopes

linkedIds.text: EA7 Emporio Armani Milan v Maccabi Rapyd Tel Aviv (2026-03-13T19:30:00.000Z)

content.text:
EA7 Emporio Armani Milan engineered a lifeline 96-87 home win over Maccabi Rapyd Tel Aviv to stay in the hunt for a play-in berth, while denting the Israeli side’s hopes of squeezing into the business end of Europe’s premier club competition.

The Italian team improved to 16-15 after grinding out its fifth win in the last seven games as Maccabi dropped to 14-16 after suffering a first defeat in five outings.

Center Josh Nebo put his former club Maccabi to the sword with a monster performance of 19 points and 10 rebounds, and Shavon Shields netted 11 of his 17 points in the fourth quarter as Milan repelled a fierce Maccabi fightback.

The visitors had slashed a 15-point third quarter deficit to just 84-80 in the home stretch before Shields sank a pair of successive three-pointers to put the game beyond Maccabi’s reach. Marko Guduric racked up 11 points and 8 assists for the home team, Zach LeDay scored 15 and Quinn Ellis added 12 as Milan gave itself a fighting chance of sneaking into the top 10 at the end of the Regular Season.

Nebo, who was a handful for his opponents all night, acknowledged that keeping their nerve when the chips were down in the fourth quarter was crucial for the home players.  

“In the past we had some trouble in the fourth quarter and today was a big step forward in responding to their run and closing the game out,” Nebo said in his postgame comments. “It was a tough game and we’re trying to push for a spot in the playoffs, so I try to bring my best effort to every matchup. The race is very tight so we’re trying to take it one game at a time and if we keep fighting like we did tonight, we’ve got a good chance,” he added.

Maccabi nosed ahead 25-26 at the end of the first quarter as Tamir Blatt beat the buzzer with an audacious three-pointer from the halfway line, but Milan then gained the upper hand and led 53-42 at halftime. The host appeared to be in for some plain sailing when it romped 59-44 ahead early in the third, but Maccabi had other ideas and always threatened to force a late twist before Shields delivered for Milan from long range.

View this post on Instagram

A post shared by Olimpia Milano (@olimpiamilano1936)

The home team’s head coach Pepe Poeta was able to breathe a huge sigh of relief on the final buzzer after seeing his men close out the game. “We had three very solid quarters and we handled the endgame in a proper way,” he said. “It’s impossible to take away everything from a team as talented on offense as Maccabi but we did a good job limiting them and with this spirit we will try to reach the postseason.”

Lonnie Walker paced the visitors on 16 points and Gur Lavi, who averaged just 3.1 points per game before Friday night, served up a big first half when he poured in 13 of his 15 points, eventually finishing the game on a perfect 7-of-7 from the floor. Roman Sorkin and Jimmy Clark scored 13 apiece for Maccabi but the visitors were ultimately undone by Milan’s sound free throw shooting, as the home team netted 27 of 30 from the foul line.

“We played against a good team and they shot the ball really well,” Maccabi head coach Oded Kattash said. “We did a better job in the second half and the most important thing is that we kept fighting. We have a week to recover and get the next one.”

articleMetadata.title: Josh Nebo slays former club as Milan beats battling Maccabi

heroMedia.title: Maccabi - Hapoel and Partizan - Dubai suspended

content.text:
Euroleague Basketball would like to inform that the following EuroLeague Regular Season Round 30 games have been suspended:

Maccabi Rapyd Tel Aviv - Hapoel IBI Tel Aviv, scheduled to be played on Thursday, March 5.

Partizan Mozzart Bet Belgrade – Dubai Basketball, scheduled to be played on Thursday, March 5.

The games have been suspended due to the current situation in the region and the resulting inability to travel following the temporary closure of the airspace. Euroleague Basketball will evaluate with the affected teams the best possible options to reschedule the games, while closely monitoring the latest developments and maintaining constant communication with local and international authorities, and all relevant organizations to ensure the safety and well-being of everyone involved. Further information will be released in the coming days.

articleMetadata.title: Hapoel IBI Tel Aviv - Paris Basket suspended
articleMetadata.description:
The EuroLeague Regular Season Round 21 game between Hapoel IBI Tel Aviv and Paris Basket that was scheduled to be played on Tuesday, March 3 in Tel Aviv has been suspended due to the current situation in the region.

Euroleague Basketball will evaluate with the affected teams the best possible options to reschedule the game. Further information will be released in the coming days.

heroMedia.title: Maccabi holds firm late to defeat Monaco on the road
heroMedia.summary: Walker scores six straight in the clutch as Maccabi claims its fourth straight win

linkedIds.text: AS Monaco v Maccabi Rapyd Tel Aviv (2026-02-26T18:30:00.000Z)

content.text:
[Maccabi Rapyd Tel Aviv](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/teams/maccabi-rapyd-tel-aviv/tel/) boosted its hopes of squeezing into the postseason, improving to 14-15 with an 86-91 win at [AS Monaco](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/teams/as-monaco/mco/) thanks to a balanced, composed performance, in which several players stood out to cancel a valiant solo display from the home side’s [Jaron Blossomgame](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/jaron-blossomgame/011231/).

The visitors led for the lion’s share of the contest, wiping out an early 11-10 deficit with a 0-11 run and maintaining control until a brief spurt by Monaco, which nosed ahead 76-75 in the fourth quarter before running out of steam during the closing stages.

Blossomgame delivered the best game of his EuroLeague career with 25 points and a PIR of 33, adding 7 boards and 2 assists, but [Alpha Diallo](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/alpha-diallo/011226/) was the only other beacon of light in a shorthanded Monaco side, adding 19 points for the hosts. [Matthew Strazel](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/matthew-strazel/008099/) and [Elie Okobo](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/elie-okobo/011212/) scored 10 each and [Mike James](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/mike-james/005985/) added 9, but the latter two combined to shoot 1‑of‑12 from deep as they epitomized a dreadful shooting night for the Roca Team, which made a mere 7‑of‑35 from downtown.

Monaco slipped to 16-13 and will now be looking over its shoulders in ninth after a sixth loss in its last seven games.

content.title: AS Monaco-Maccabi Rapyd Tel Aviv | Round 29 Highlights | 2025-26 EuroLeague

content.text:
In contrast, a steady Maccabi outfit was led by [Lonnie Walker](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/lonnie-walker-iv/013403/) on 18 points, while [Roman Sorkin](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/roman-sorkin/011219/) and [Jaylen Hoard](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/jaylen-hoard/006835/) chipped in with 15 each, the latter also racking up 7 rebounds. [Oshae Brissett](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/o-shae-j-brissett/014127/) added 12 points as the visiting team dished out 22 assists and won the battle of the boards 37-33, hitting 12‑of‑35 from deep.

Coach Oded Kattash stressed it was a morale-boosting win for Maccabi, which kept its nerve in a tense finish as Walker scored 6 successive points to keep Monaco at bay after the hosts had threatened to force the final twist.

“We showed character and it’s a very special win for us in a tough place,” Kattash said in his postgame comments. “It’s tough to play against Monaco defensively and we did a good job, while keeping our focus offensively and it was a team effort. We started the game really well and kept believing in tough moments.”

View this post on Instagram

A post shared by Maccabi Tel Aviv Basketball (@maccabitlv)

Hoard, who carried Maccabi offensively in the first half when he netted 13 points, was just as upbeat as he summed up the Israeli side’s campaign spent far from home due to security conditions that forced the team to host games in Belgrade in a largely empty gym. He also emphasized that Maccabi has worked hard to hold on in crunch time after suffering several tough losses this term.

“Throughout the season we’ve been in games like this where we let the lead slip away and lost, but we’ve learned from our mistakes in the past and were able to get the win today,” he said. “The chemistry is bringing us closer and closer together each and every day after some hard times we’ve been through.”

Having thrived at one point when it was top of the table, Monaco has plunged down the standings and its campaign is threatening to spiral out of control as pack leader James seems to be struggling, while coach Vassilis Spanoulis tried to put on a brave face after yet another unconvincing display.

“We were out of energy as we only had 11 players available after having to play five games in a week, but it’s not an excuse,” the Greek tactician said. “They played better, so congratulations to them for the win. The first period was a disaster for us as we conceded 30 points. The next three periods we played well, but once again our defense was not good as we conceded (in excess of) 90 points.”

linkedId.text: AS Monaco v Maccabi Rapyd Tel Aviv (2026-02-26T18:30:00.000Z)

articleMetadata.title: Maccabi holds firm late to defeat Monaco on the road
articleMetadata.description: Maccabi Rapyd Tel Aviv earned a crucial 86–91 road victory at AS Monaco to extend its winning streak to four and move to 14–15. Despite a career night from Monaco’s Jaron Blossomgame (25 points, PIR 33), Maccabi controlled most of the game and sealed the win behind Lonnie Walker’s late scoring burst. Roman Sorkin and Jaylen Hoard added 15 each, while the visitors won the rebounding battle and knocked down 12 threes. Coaches Oded Kattash and Vassilis Spanoulis reflected on their teams’ contrasting trajectories in the playoff race.

heroMedia.title: Phase 2 general public tickets for the 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens presented by Etihad sell out

content.text:
The extraordinary demand for the 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens Presented by Etihad shows no signs of slowing down, as all tickets made available during Phase 2 of public sales have officially sold out.

When Phase 2 opened on February 26 at 10:00 CET, fans once again responded in massive numbers. Following Phase 1, which saw more than one million fans connect to the ticketing platform and resulted in a complete sellout, the second allocation was also quickly exhausted, reaffirming the immense appeal of European basketball’s premier event.

The overwhelming global interest highlights the unique atmosphere and significance of the Final Four, with fans from across Europe and beyond eager to secure their place in Athens for the season’s ultimate showdown.

Fans who were unable to purchase tickets during General public tickets sale Phase 2 will still have an additional opportunity on February 26 as VIP tickets will be available starting at 14:00 CET.

Fans who were unable to secure tickets during Phase 1 will still have multiple opportunities to attend European basketball’s premier event. The second public ticket sales phase will open on February 26 at 10:00 CET. The third public ticket sales phase will open on March 17 at 10:00 CET, while the final sales phase will begin on April 20 at 10:00 CEST, coinciding with the conclusion of the Regular Season as teams continue their pursuit of a Final Four berth.

Each remaining phase will feature a limited allocation across multiple price categories, and high demand is expected once again.

All ticketing details for the 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens Presented by Etihad are available at [https://euroleaguef4tickets.com/en/](https://euroleaguef4tickets.com/en/).

heroMedia.title: Power rankings: After the cups have gone
heroMedia.summary: Our experts offer up a suggestion for every team through 28 rounds

content.text:
Welcome to the latest edition of the EuroLeague Power Rankings. The list is the brainchild of [EuroLeague.net](http://EuroLeague.net)'s team of experts, which is made up of Geoff Gillingham, Javier Gancedo, Frankie Sachs and Igor Petrinovic. The rankings are not a prediction of what the final standings will look like, but rather where all 20 teams stand – in their humble opinions – today, after Round 28.

 

### 1. Fenerbahce Beko Istanbul (20-7)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Besiktas on Sunday to win the Turkish Cup

**The question we are asking:** Is Fenerbahce aware that not team to finish the regular season in first place under the current format has won the championship?

 

### 2. Olympiacos Piraeus (18-9)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Panathinaikos in the Greek Cup final on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** Do we need to start worrying about the health of the Reds’ stars?

 

### 3. Real Madrid (17-11)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Baskonia in the Spanish Cup final on Sunday

**The question we are asking:** Can *Los Blancos* improve its defense to become a top contender?

### 4. Panathinaikos AKTOR Athens (16-12)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Olympiacos to win the Greek Cup on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** Will the addition of Nigel Hayes-Davis be enough to put the Greens over the top?

 

### 5. Valencia Basket (18-10)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Real Madrid in the Spanish Cup semifinal on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** How will Valencia shake its disappointment from not winning the Spanish Cup at home? 

 

### 6. FC Barcelona (17-11)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Barcelona in the Spanish Cup semifinal on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** With Punter back in action, can Barça improve its consistency and stay in the top-six race?

 

### 7. Zalgiris Kaunas (16-12)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Rytas Vilnius in the Lithuanian Cup final on Sunday

**The question we are asking:** With six home games remaining and good momentum, is a top-six finish a realistic goal?

### 8. AS Monaco (16-12)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Le Mans to win the French Leaders Cup on Sunday

**The question we are asking:** After a turbulent stretch on and off the court, can this trophy galvanize Monaco and stabilize its EuroLeague push?

 

### 9. Hapoel IBI Tel Aviv (16-11)

**What they did last week:** Went 3-0 in the Israeli League

**The question we are asking:** Is Hapoel’s recent slide just a bump in the road or a sign that the team peaked too early?

 

### 10. Crvena Zvezda Meridianbet Belgrade (16-12)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Mega Belgrade to win the Serbian Cup on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** Is Jordan Nwora the star who can carry Zvezda to new heights? 

 

### 11. Dubai Basketball (14-14)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Bosna Sarajevo in the ABA League

**The question we are asking:** Now that Dzanan Musa is back and in shape, can Dubai win enough games to make it to the Play-In Showdown?

### 12. Maccabi Rapyd Tel Aviv (13-15)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Bnei Herzliya to win the Israeli Cup on Thursday

**The question we are asking:** Is there enough time left in the season for Maccabi to climb to 10th place?

 

### 13. EA7 Emporio Armani Milan (14-14)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Tortona to win the Italian Cup on Sunday

**The question we are asking:** Can Milan translate domestic momentum into EuroLeague consistency and revive its play-in hopes?

 

### 14. FC Bayern Munich (12-16)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Bamberg in German Cup semifinals on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** What would this team have looked like with a full season under Coach Pesic?

 

### 15. Anadolu Efes Istanbul (9-19)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Besiktas in the Turkish Cup semifinal on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** Is Efes destined to become a spoiler for a few teams in the playoff race?

### 16. Virtus Bologna (12-16)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Tortona in the Italian Cup semifinals on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** What adjustments can Dusko Ivanovic make to halt Virtus’s slide – especially away from home?

 

### 17. Partizan Mozzart Bet Belgrade (9-19)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Mega Belgrade in the Serbian Cup semifinals on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** Can Joan Penarroya realign the locker room and restore Partizan’s competitive edge?

 

### 18. Paris Basketball (9-18)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Bourg in the French Leader’s Cup quarterfinals on Friday

**The question we are asking:** What will it take for Paris to recapture its early-season identity and get Nadir Hifi back to his best?

 

### 19. Kosner Baskonia Vitoria-Gasteiz (9-19)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Real Madrid in the Spanish Cup final on Sunday

**The question we are asking:** After its Spanish Cup success and strong home performances, can Baskonia start winning consistently on the road?

 

### 20. LDLC ASVEL Villeurbanne (7-21)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Monaco in the French Cup quarterfinals on Tuesday

**The question we are asking:** With the postseason out of reach, which players are auditioning for their EuroLeague futures?

articleMetadata.title: Power rankings: After the cups have gone
articleMetadata.description: Welcome to this post-domestic cups edition of the EuroLeague Power Rankings. The list is the brainchild of EuroLeague.net's team of experts, which is made up of Geoff Gillingham, Javier Gancedo, Frankie Sachs and Igor Petrinovic.

heroMedia.title: Playmaker Amit Ebo returns home to Maccabi
heroMedia.summary: The former youth team product returns home following standout campaigns in Slovakia, Lithuania and France

content.text:
Maccabi Rapyd Tel Aviv fortified its backcourt by signing Israeli playmaker [Amit Ebo](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/amit-ebo/006020/), the club announced on Tuesday. The contract is through the end of the 2026-27 season. Ebo (1.84 meters, 26 years old) arrives from Boulazac, where he has averaged 11.0 points and 3.1 assists in 15 French League games this season. He scored a season-high 21 against AS Monaco in October.

A product of the Maccabi youth academy, Ebo returns to Israel after two and a half seasons abroad. After standing out with 22.1 points with Komarno in the Slovakian League in 2023-24, he moved to Lithuania mid-season and the following campaign was the Lithuanian League leader in assists with 8.5 along with 16.4 points and 3.5 rebounds in 26 games for Nevezis. That led to a deal with Boulazac this season.

Ebo made his professional debut with Maccabi in April 2017 and spent the next season with Eilat. Then came half a season with Rishon Lezion, a season and a half with Maccabi Ashdod. He played the 2020-21 campaign with Ironi Ness Ziona and a season each with Hapoel Ramat Gan and Ramat Hasharon in Israel’s second division before heading abroad. Ebo's trophy case includes winning the 2020-21 FIBA Europe Cup with Ness Ziona and the 2018 Israeli League Cup with Rishon Lezion.​ He has also played for Israel at the U16 and U18 levels and now returns home to make his EuroLeague debut with Maccabi.

כל הפרטים >> https://t.co/kIFIvV1qsZ pic.twitter.com/geugiRPrfA

— Maccabi Tel Aviv BC (@MaccabiTLVBC) February 24, 2026

articleMetadata.title: Playmaker Amit Ebo returns home to Maccabi
articleMetadata.description: Maccabi Rapyd Tel Aviv fortified its backcourt by signing Israeli guard Amit Ebo to a multi year contract. Ebo arrives from Boulazac after averaging 11.0 points and 3.1 assists in the French League and returns to Israel following productive stints in Slovakia and Lithuania, including leading the Lithuanian League in assists.

heroMedia.title: 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens presented by Etihad – Ticket sales Phase 2 to start on February 26
heroMedia.summary: Phase 1 general public ticket sale attracted over 1 million fans worldwide

content.text:
Ticket sales for the 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens presented by Etihad will enter their next chapter on February 26 at 10:00 CET, as Phase 2 opens to the general public.

The launch of Phase 2 coincides with the closing of the EuroLeague players’ market, ensuring that fans will have a clear picture of each team’s final roster.

All fans,  except those who have already purchased tickets during previous sales phases or pre-sales , will be eligible to access the February 26 general public sale starting at 10:00 CET. During this second sales window,  all fans will also have the opportunity to purchase VIP tickets starting at 14:00 CET .

Ticketing information is available at [https://euroleaguef4tickets.com/en/](https://euroleaguef4tickets.com/en/). Fans must log in or create a EuroLeague ID account to complete their purchase. Once logged in, all available categories will be displayed. Due to expected high demand and to ensure fairness, each fan may purchase a maximum of two tickets.

For the first time, Final Four tickets are being released in multiple sales phases. This approach is designed to expand access for the EuroLeague’s growing global fan base, offering multiple opportunities to secure tickets,  whether fans plan to attend regardless of the qualified teams or prefer to wait until the competition reaches its decisive stages. All phases follow a fair access policy, meaning that fans who purchase tickets in one phase will not be eligible to participate in subsequent phases. This measure is designed to ensure broader access to the event.

Phase 1, held on February 11, experienced massive demand, as over a million fans connected to sold out all available tickets in less than five minutes, once again demonstrating the enormous appeal of the EuroLeague’s premier event.

The 2026 EuroLeague Final Four semifinals will be played on Friday, May 22, at 18:00 and 21:00 local time (17:00 and 20:00 CEST). On Sunday, May 24, the adidas NextGen EuroLeague Championship Game will tip off at 18:00 local time (17:00 CEST), while the season will culminate with the Championship Game at 21:00 local time (20:00 CEST), when a new EuroLeague champion will be crowned.

A third ticket sales window will open on Tuesday, March 17, at 10:00 CET, offering another opportunity for fans to secure their seats. The final sales phase will launch on Monday, April 20, at 10:00 CEST, positioned between the conclusion of the Regular Season and the start of the Play-In Showdown, with 10 teams still in contention for a place in the Final Four.

All ticketing details for the 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens presented by Etihad are available at [https://euroleaguef4tickets.com/en/](https://euroleaguef4tickets.com/en/).

articleMetadata.title: 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens presented by Etihad – Ticket sales Phase 2 to start on February 26
articleMetadata.description: Ticket sales for the 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens presented by Etihad will enter their next chapter on February 26 at 10:00 CET, as Phase 2 opens to the general public.

heroMedia.title: Maccabi lifts Israel’s State Cup for record 47th time
heroMedia.summary: Roman Sorkin was named finals MVP after a dominant first half and a total of 25 points

content.text:
Maccabi Rapyd Tel Aviv celebrated winning Israel’s State Cup for a record 47th time on Thursday after beating Bnei Herzliya 109-90 in the final at Menora Mivtachim Arena. Maccabi never trailed after the opening minute, led by 20 at halftime and was never threatened in the second half.

Roman Sorkin powered Maccabi early with 19 first-half points en route to a game-high 25 and earn finals MVP honors. Jaylen Hoard added 20 points on near-perfect shooting, Tamir Blatt paired 14 points with 8 assists, Jimmy Clark tallied 11 points and 8 dimes and Marcio Santos also scored 11. Chinanu Onuaku led Herzliya with 16 points, 8 rebounds and 6 assists and Noah Carter added 15 points in defeat.

Frederic Bourdillon opened the scoring with a three-pointer for Herzliya, but Maccabi bounced back with a 12-2 run in which Hoard scored half the points. Max Heidegger came off the bench and struck 3 times from deep to bring Herzliya within 22-21. Gur Lavy helped Maccabi push ahead to 30-23 at the end of the opening quarter. A triple by Jon DiBartolomeo gave Maccabi its first double-digit lead on the first possession of the second quarter. Maccabi held Herzliya to just a single basket in the first 4 minutes of the quarter, but missed layups kept it from capitalizing. When Roman Sorkin stepped up and knocked down a trio of threes, the Maccabi lead started to swell and it was 59-39 at halftime.

Herzliya went inside to Onuaku at the onset of the second half to try and ignite its offense, but could only trim the deficit to 16 before Maccabi got going again. The margin remained in the neighborhood of 20 points for the rest of the third quarter, which finished with Maccabi in control, 83-63. Blatt drilled a three to take Maccabi past the 100-point plateau with more than 4 minutes to go. The margin peaked at 27 – on three occasions – as the Maccabi fans started celebrating long before the final buzzer.

articleMetadata.title: Maccabi lifts Israel’s State Cup for record 47th time
articleMetadata.description: Maccabi Rapyd Tel Aviv celebrated winning Israel’s State Cup for a record 47th time on Thursday after beating Bnei Herzliya 109-90 in the final at Menora Mivtachim Arena.

heroMedia.title: Maccabi races past Jerusalem late to reach Israeli Cup final
heroMedia.summary: Sorkin and Dowtin combined for 39 points and Hoard provided the final push

content.text:
They call it Israel’s “Superclassic” and for good reason. Hapoel Midtown Jerusalem led for most of the game, but Jaylen Hoard scored 7 points in an 11-0 run late in the fourth quarter that gave Maccabi Rapyd Tel Aviv the lead for good en route to a 94-85 victory over the Jerusalemites in the first semifinal of the Israeli Cup on Sunday at Menora Mivtachim Arena. Maccabi will face the winner of Monday’s semifinal between Hapoel Gilboa Galil and Bnei Herzliya in Thursday’s final.

Roman Sorkin scored 20 points to lead Maccabi, of which 15 came in the first half. Jeff Dowtin, in his first meaningful minutes in more than two months, added 19. Jimmy Clark tallied 16 points and 5 assists and Hoard finished with 13 points for the winners. Khadeen Carrington paced the Jerusalem attack with 22 points, Austin Wiley amassed 17 points, 13 rebounds and 3 blocks and Jared Harper paired 13 points and 8 assists.

מכבי בגמר הגביע!
סיקור וסטטיסטיקה >> https://t.co/h6eOeUWs0Y pic.twitter.com/0sfuuVd9bh

— Maccabi Tel Aviv BC (@MaccabiTLVBC) February 15, 2026

Jerusalem scored the first 7 points and led 9-19 after a Cassius Winston three. Sorkin, who had 8 first-quarter points, helped Maccabi get within 17-21 and the first 10 minutes ended with the scoreboard showing 19-24. Jerusalem’s guards pushed the margin to 21-33, but Maccabi roared back on a Dowtin-led 15-2 run to grab its first lead, 40-39. Harper nailed a three-pointer to make it 45-47 at the break

Yovel Zoosman hit a pair of threes and Justin Smith became an active force as Jerusalem slowly pushed its lead to 59-68. Maccabi clawed its way back with 7 unanswered points, including a Jimmy Clark three on the third-quarter buzzer to make it 66-68. Gur Lavy tied it with the first basket of the fourth quarter. Mobley and Carrington soon had Jerusalem back in control, though Maccabi remained hot in pursuit. Clark uncorked a monster slam to even things at 76-76 with less than 5 minutes remaining and then followed it up with a tough layup to give his team the lead. However, Hoard led Maccabi’s game-changing run to take it to the final.

articleMetadata.title: Maccabi races past Jerusalem late to reach Israeli Cup final
articleMetadata.description: Maccabi Rapyd Tel Aviv rallied past Hapoel Midtown Jerusalem 94-85 in the Israeli Cup semifinal, fueled by a late 11-0 run led by Jaylen Hoard.
